untranslatable_reason: This signal describes a microstructure trading strategy based on soliciting latent demand from counterparties. The core logic depends on: (1) order submission cost thresholds in basis points, (2) counterparty database lookups and trading history, (3) bid-ask spread conditions, and (4) contact attempt tracking. None of these require price action indicators. The allowed indicators (RSI, MACD, moving averages, etc.) are technical chart indicators unsuitable for this fundamental liquidity/counterparty-based strategy. The entry/exit logic is driven by operational trading decisions (actively contacting counterparties, order execution obtained, contact attempt limits) rather than market price/volume conditions. This cannot be expressed as a technical rules engine without counterparty database, cost, and contact tracking data structures.
translation_notes: Signal requires: order_cost_threshold_bps (1 bps), spread_threshold_bps (0), min_block_size_shares (100), lookback_period_months (24), max_contacts_per_block (10). Exit on: order execution, 30 min elapsed, max contacts reached. These are operational/transactional parameters, not technical indicators. Recommend implementing as separate counterparty solicitation module outside the technical backtest engine.
